title: Weather Vocabulary Audit — Final Results

urban-light.json.quality: EXCELLENT — vivid city-specific lighting (neon, sodium-vapour, LED, café glow)
urban-light.json.notes: urbanLight field is numeric (float), lightCharacter is the phrase list. 83 cities × 3 phrases each.

conditions.json.quality: VERY GOOD — weather condition descriptors across 14 types + 12 contextual
conditions.json.notes: Removed 12 phrases: technical terms (°C references, saturation levels)

wind.json.quality: POOR RAW — entirely formulaic ("[prefix] air movement" × 30 ranges × 8 templates)
wind.json.notes: Reduced from 240 to 16 hand-crafted replacements. Original phrases are for weather generator, not humans.

humidity.json.quality: MIXED — some visual, some too meteorological
humidity.json.notes: Removed generic labels like "low humidity", "moderate humidity", kept "air thick with moisture"

time-of-day.json.quality: GOOD — time period labels and descriptors
time-of-day.json.notes: Light-related ones routed to lighting, time labels to atmosphere

city-vibes.json.quality: EXCELLENT — real venues from 102 cities worldwide
city-vibes.json.notes: Kept "Venue, City" format only (1,032). Dropped 986 standalone names without city context.

environment.source: city-vibes.json venues (102 cities × 10 venues, "Venue, City" format)

quality_decisions: KEPT: >8 word lighting phrases — they are vivid and descriptive, not bloated
KEPT: 5 single-word time descriptors (midnight, midday, etc.) — valid atmosphere terms
REMOVED: All 240 wind.json phrases — entirely formulaic templates, replaced with 16 hand-crafted
REMOVED: All standalone venue names — "Heritage Village" without city context is ambiguous
REMOVED: All "X humidity" labels — meteorological, not atmospheric description
